mission.title: helm: Feature Request: helm delete --wait
mission.description: Feature Request: helm delete --wait. Requested by 111+ users.

steps.title: Check current helm deployment
steps.description:
Verify your helm version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n helm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=helm
helm list -n helm 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working helm installation.

steps.title: Review helm configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant helm configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n helm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=helm
kubectl get configmap -n helm -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=helm
```
`helm install --wait` can wait for everything to be ready. Can a new `helm delete --wait` do something similar? I'm using `helm delete --purge` in my build pipeline followed by this script (to do something similar to `--wait`).

steps.title: Apply the fix for Feature Request: helm delete --wait
steps.description:
Without https://github.com/helm/helm/issues/2378 there isn't a way to
deterministically know when all resources are finished being removed. A
simple thing we can do is warn the user on a ES_FORCE_INSTALL=true.
```yaml
#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -euo pipefail

main() {
	local kinds=( Service Deployment Pod Secret ConfigMap )
	local counter=0 attempts=30

	for release in "$@"; do
		echo "--> Deleting release ${release}"

		if helm ls --kube-context "${KUBE_CONTEXT}" | grep -qF "${release}"; then
			echo "==> Found helm release; deleting with --purge"
			helm delete "${release}" --purge  --kube-context "${KUBE_CONTEXT}"
		else
			echo "==> No release found; deleting manually"

			for kind in "${kinds[@]}"; do
				echo "==> Deleting any dangling ${kind}"

				kubectl delete "${kind}" \
					-
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n helm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=helm
kubectl get events -n helm --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Feature Request: helm delete --wait" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
⚠️ Warning: The workaround script uses `--force --grace-period 0` which bypasses graceful termination and can cause data loss or resource leaks. Use with caution in production. Without https://github.com/helm/helm/issues/2378 there isn't a way to
deterministically know when all resources are finished being removed. A
simple thing we can do is warn the user on a ES_FORCE_INSTALL=true.
codeSnippets:
#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -euo pipefail

main() {
	local kinds=( Service Deployment Pod Secret ConfigMap )
	local counter=0 attempts=30

	for release in "$@"; do
		echo "--> Deleting release ${release}"

		if helm ls --kube-context "${KUBE_CONTEXT}" | grep -qF "${release}"; then
			echo "==> Found helm release; deleting with --purge"
			helm delete "${release}" --purge  --kube-context "${KUBE_CONTEXT}"
		else
			echo "==> No release found; deleting manually"

			for kind in "${kinds[@]}"; do
				echo "==> Deleting any dangling ${kind}"

				kubectl delete "${kind}" \
					-l "release=${release}" \
					-n "${KUBE_NAMESPACE}" \
					--force \
					--grace-period 0 \
					--context "${KUBE_CONTEXT}" 2>/dev/null
			done
		fi

		echo "--> Awaiting resource deleting

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with helm installed or the issue environment reproducible.
